title: Primary Purpose of a Work Breakdown Structure (WBS)
summary: Describes why a Work Breakdown Structure is created and how it supports project planning and control.
content: The primary purpose of the Work Breakdown Structure (WBS) is to provide a hierarchical decomposition of the total scope of work required to produce the project’s deliverables. It establishes a clear, deliverable‑oriented view of what will be produced, serving as the foundation for creating schedules, budgets, network diagrams, and scope‑creep control. The WBS does not contain activities, cost estimates, resource assignments, or dependencies; those are derived from the WBS later in the planning process.
can_answer: What is the primary purpose of a Work Breakdown Structure?
Why should a WBS be created before developing a project schedule?
